subject: Public Health
title: Tolerance, Dependence, and Withdrawal
description: A short lesson to help you apply Tolerance, Dependence, and Withdrawal.

hook.text: First one drink relaxes you. Then you need three. Then you can't function without them. That's the progression.

buildup.text: Tolerance means the body needs increasing doses to achieve the same effect—neurons adapt to the substance's presence.

discovery.text: Physical dependence follows—the body cannot function normally without the substance and reacts violently to its absence.

twist.text: Alcohol withdrawal can be fatal—seizures and delirium tremens kill more people than withdrawal from heroin.

climax.text: Medically supervised detoxification manages withdrawal safely—but it's only the first step in recovery.

punchline.text: Tolerance adapts. Dependence demands. Withdrawal rebels.

quiz.question: What is tolerance in the context of substance use?
options: Needing increasing doses for the same effect
Choosing not to use a substance
